name: E. Glen Weyl

name: Alison Gopnik

notes: Leading developmental psychologist who applies her research on children's learning to critique current AI systems, particularly LLMs. Argues AI systems are 'cultural technologies' like printing presses rather than intelligent agents, and that true AI intelligence would require embodied interaction with the world. name: Divya Siddarth

notes: Founded Collective Intelligence Project to develop democratic governance models for AI. Led alignment assemblies with Taiwan's Digital Ministry that informed deepfake legislation. Co-authored Harvard Law Review piece on co-governance for AI regulation. Named to TIME100 AI list and served as TED speaker on AI and democracy.

name: Samantha Bradshaw
title: Assistant Professor in New Technology & Security, American University; Director, Center for Security, Innovation and New Technology; CIGI Fellow
notes: Leading expert on computational propaganda and disinformation who authored influential Oxford Internet Institute reports tracking organized social media manipulation in 70+ countries. Her research on 'identity-based disinformation' examines how racism, sexism, and xenophobia are weaponized online to incite violence. name: Walter Isaacson

name: Catherine Cortez Masto

name: Buck Shlegeris
notes: CEO of Redwood Research, an AI safety nonprofit focused on AI control — ensuring safe deployment of potentially misaligned AI. Co-founder of Redwood and board member at ARC. name: Lisa Hansmann

name: Angela Virtu

name: Richard Phillips

name: Cecilia Elena Tilli

name: Goda Mockutė

name: Santiago Rodriguez

name: James Chua

name: Samuel Martin
notes: AI safety researcher who co-authored the Conditional AI Safety Treaty paper with Rebecca Scholefield and the Existential Risk Observatory. Listed as unaffiliated in the publication. The paper proposes an international framework for pausing AI development when safety institutes determine risks are unacceptable, with compute thresholds and mandatory audits overseen by AI Safety Institutes.

notes: Maduraperuma/Khot Professor of Computer Science at Princeton; director of Princeton Precision Health. Also deputy director of Genomics at the Flatiron Institute (Simons Foundation) since 2014. PhD in Biomedical Informatics from Stanford. Research builds AI methods to decode genomes and diagnose/treat complex diseases, focusing on the non-coding genome and multi-omics modeling. Affiliated with Lewi

name: Matt Fredrikson
notes: Associate Professor, Carnegie Mellon School of Computer Science (ECE). Co-founder and CEO of Gray Swan AI. Research: secure, fair, reliable ML — adversarial robustness, privacy, formal methods. Pioneered methods to identify LLM vulnerabilities. Ran largest public red-teaming competition: 22 frontier AI agents, 44 deployment scenarios, 1.8M prompt-injection attacks, 60K+ successful policy violation

name: Judd Rosenblatt
notes: Founder and CEO of AE Studio (est. 2016), a bootstrapped 150+ person company focused on software development, brain-computer interfaces, and AI alignment research. Left a food delivery startup to work on ensuring AI safety. Engages with National Security Council, White House OSTP, congressional staff, and frontier AI lab founders on AI policy. Leads AE Studio's 'neglected approaches' alignment age

name: Claire Zabel
notes: Managing Director, Short Timelines Special Projects at Coefficient Giving (formerly Open Philanthropy). Leads a team researching and implementing initiatives on the assumption transformative AI may arrive soon. Previously ran Global Catastrophic Risks Capacity Building (2018-2024) and oversaw Biosecurity & Pandemic Preparedness (2018-2019, 2023-2024). Former Research Analyst at GiveWell. B.S. and
